intro: Nativex and Teads overlap on scores and pricing posture, so the decision comes down to which problem each was actually built for. Nativex is native and in-app advertising network, mainly for user acquisition. Teads is outstream video ad marketplace for premium publishers. Pick Nativex when the job is publishers expanding into china and emerging markets needing a local-savvy partner and you accept high lock-in; pick Teads when it is content publishers monetizing mobile web video with brand-safe premium demand and you accept high lock-in. The data we have shows Nativex at opaque pricing and product information with average developer experience, and Teads at opaque pricing and product information with average developer experience. The honest trade-off: neither is universal — Nativex is a poor fit for western enterprises with strict china-related vendor review policies, and Teads is a poor fit for mobile game developers seeking performance-driven rewarded or interstitial fill. Match the pricing model and lock-in level to how your team actually works, not the feature list.
quick_take: Nativex is for native and in-app advertising network; Teads is for outstream video ad marketplace; decide based on whether you can accept sales-call pricing.
verdict_a: Choose Nativex if you can absorb a custom-quote sales cycle and the work maps to native and in-app advertising network, mainly for user acquisition.
verdict_b: Choose Teads if deep integration is an acceptable price for the depth it buys and the work lines up with outstream video ad marketplace for premium publishers.

faq.a: Neither Nativex nor Teads publishes entry pricing that lets us compare directly — Nativex uses a custom model and Teads uses a custom model. Get quotes from both before assuming one is cheaper.

faq.q: Can I migrate from Nativex to Teads?
faq.a: Moving from Nativex to Teads is feasible, but Teads is rated high lock-in — expect your integrations and workflows to need rework to match its model. Budget for a parallel-run period rather than a hard cutover.

Both Nativex and Teads rate the same on developer experience (3/5). The decision on DX then comes down to taste — which CLI, UI, or workflow matches your team's habits. A short side-by-side trial is the quickest way to tell.

faq.q: Is Teads a good alternative to Nativex?
faq.a: Yes — Teads is a reasonable alternative to Nativex for publishers expanding into china and emerging markets needing a local-savvy partner.. The practical differences are custom-vs-custom billing and high-vs-high lock-in. If those fit your constraints better, treat Teads as a credible swap.
